text: Writing clean code is essential for creating software that is easy to understand and modify. The most important goal is to make your code readable, as if you were writing for another person. One simple way to achieve this is by following naming conventions. Use clear and descriptive names for variables, functions, and classes. Avoid short, meaningless names like "x" or "temp." Additionally, break your code into small, focused functions that each do one thing well. This approach improves maintainability, meaning other developers—or even you in the future—can quickly fix bugs or add new features without confusion. Clean code saves time and prevents errors.
